easy.title: Basic Canonical Tag Implementation
easy.description: An e-commerce site has a product page accessible at multiple URLs due to tracking parameters. Implement a proper canonical tag to consolidate all versions to the base product URL.
easy.starterCode:
Premium Running Shoes

Price: $129.99

Category: Footwear

You arrived via:
easy.solution:
Premium Running Shoes

Price: $129.99

Category: Footwear

You arrived via:

medium.title: Multi-Scenario Canonical Strategy
medium.description: You have a blog website with multiple duplicate content issues. Fix all of them: (1) The printer-friendly version at /printable/article-slug, (2) The session ID variant at /article-slug?session=abc123, (3) The mobile subdomain at m.example.com/article-slug, (4) The category/tag archive that overlaps with another tag, (5) Add self-referencing canonical for the main article page. Create the complete <head> section for the main article page with all appropriate canonical tags and related SEO meta tags.
medium.starterCode:
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>10 HTML Best Practices - My Blog</title>
  <!-- TODO: Add SEO meta tags -->
  
  <!-- TODO: Add canonical tags -->
  
  <!-- TODO: Add mobile alternate tag -->
  
</head>
medium.solution:
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>10 HTML Best Practices - My Blog</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Learn the top 10 HTML best practices for writing clean, semantic, and accessible web pages.">
  
  <!-- Self-referencing canonical (main article page) -->
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/blog/10-html-best-practices" />
  
  <!-- Mobile alternate -->
  <link rel="alternate" media="only screen and (max-width: 640px)" href="https://m.example.com/blog/10-html-best-practices" />
  
  <!-- AMP alternate -->
  <link rel="amphtml" href="https://example.com/amp/blog/10-html-best-practices" />
</head>

<!-- Note: The printer-friendly version and session-ID variant
     pages themselves include the canonical pointing to the main URL above -->

hard.title: Complete Canonical Architecture for an E-Commerce Site
hard.description: Design and implement a complete canonical URL strategy for a shoe e-commerce site. The site has: (1) Product pages with color/size variants, (2) Category pages with faceted navigation (brand, size, color, price range filters), (3) Search result pages, (4) Paginated category pages (page/2/, page/3/), (5) WWW vs non-WWW, (6) HTTP vs HTTPS. Create a configuration document showing the logic for canonical URL generation, and implement the canonical tags for each scenario with PHP-like templating logic.
hard.starterCode:
<!-- Configuration Logic for Canonical URLs -->
<?php
// Define the base canonical URL pattern for each page type
$canonical_rules = [
  'product' => '',  // TODO: Define product canonical logic
  'category' => '', // TODO: Define category facet canonical logic
  'search' => '',   // TODO: Define search page canonical logic
  'pagination' => '' // TODO: Define pagination canonical logic
];
?>

<!-- Example: Canonical tag for a filtered category page -->
<!-- URL: https://example.com/shoes?brand=nike&size=10&color=black&sort=price -->
<link rel="canonical" href="" /> <!-- TODO -->
hard.solution:
<?php
// Canonical URL Configuration

function getCanonicalUrl($pageType, $params) {
  $baseUrl = 'https://example.com';
  
  switch ($pageType) {
    case 'product':
      // Product variants canonical to base product if content is similar
      if (isset($params['color']) || isset($params['size'])) {
        // Check if variant has unique content (images, description)
        if ($params['hasUniqueContent']) {
          // Self-canonical - each variant is its own canonical
          return $baseUrl . '/products/' . $params['slug'] . '/variant/' . $params['variantId'];
        } else {
          // Canonical to base product
          return $baseUrl . '/products/' . $params['slug'];
        }
      }
      return $baseUrl . '/products/' . $params['slug'];
      
    case 'category':
      // Faceted navigation: canonical to clean category URL
      $cleanUrl = $baseUrl . '/' . $params['categorySlug'];
      
      // If only a single, important filter is applied, keep it
      $significantFilters = ['brand', 'gender'];
      $appliedFilters = array_keys($params['filters'] ?? []);
      
      foreach ($appliedFilters as $filter) {
        if (!in_array($filter, $significantFilters)) {
          // Insignificant filter - strip it
          return $cleanUrl;
        }
      }
      
      // Only significant filters: keep the combination
      if (!empty($appliedFilters)) {
        return $cleanUrl . '?' . http_build_query($params['filters']);
      }
      
      return $cleanUrl;
      
    case 'search':
      // Search results should use noindex + self-canonical
      // or canonical to a dedicated search landing page
      return $baseUrl . '/search?q=' . urlencode($params['query']);
      
    case 'pagination':
      // Paginated category pages: self-canonical OR canonical to page 1
      // If each page has substantial unique content: self-canonical
      // If thin content: canonical to page 1
      if ($params['hasThinContent']) {
        return $baseUrl . '/' . $params['categorySlug'];
      }
      return $baseUrl . '/' . $params['categorySlug'] . '/page/' . $params['pageNum'];
      
    default:
      return $baseUrl . $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'];
  }
}
?>

<!-- Implementation Examples -->

<!-- Product page with color variant that has unique images -->
<!-- URL: /products/running-shoes?color=red -->
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/products/running-shoes" />

<!-- Filtered category page: /shoes?brand=nike&size=10&color=black -->
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/shoes" />

<!-- Gender-only filter: /shoes?gender=men -->
<!-- (Gender is a 'significant' filter) -->
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/shoes?gender=men" />

<!-- Paginated page with unique content on each page -->
<!-- URL: /shoes/page/2/ -->
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/shoes/page/2/" />

<!-- Search results page -->
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/search?q=running+shoes" />
<!-- Additionally: <meta name="robots" content="noindex, follow" /> -->
